doc_title: van Berg, Kowalski & Wright (2025). Learning-Styles-Matched Instruction: A Meta-Analysis of 124 Studies. Educational Psychology Review, 37(3), 612-651.
label: fabricated
reason: This meta-analysis contradicts decades of peer-reviewed research showing learning-styles theory lacks empirical support, and such a reversal of scientific consensus would be extraordinary and widely publicized.
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 92
REASON: This meta-analysis contradicts the established scientific consensus that learning-styles theory lacks empirical support; major meta-analyses (Pashler et al. 2008, Corno & Snow 1986) found no credible evidence for matching instruction to learning styles, and the positive effect size reported here is inconsistent with peer-reviewed literature and would represent a major reversal of educational science consensus that would be widely publicized.
